name: Sentra
what: Bi-temporal context graph ('organizational memory') syncing 200+ work tools (Slack, Gmail, Drive, HubSpot, Linear, GitHub, Notion, Asana); surfaces what's true/owed/meant via NL query + MCP/REST memory API with source attribution
why_tier: Astell's exact mechanics — cross-tool ingest, graph-not-snapshot, cite-not-hallucinate, open-loop surfacing — wrapped in 'memory API' vocabulary; vocabulary never overrides mechanics (cf. Coworker)
notes: a16z Speedrun + Together Fund backed (~$5M seed reported); paid POC reported with SoftBank; HQ/founding unconfirmed. Distinct from data-security firm sentra.io

name: PipesHub
what: Open-source + commercial enterprise workplace AI: cross-SaaS search + agentic automation over Slack, Notion, GitHub, Salesforce, Drive, Jira; knowledge-graph retrieval with pinpointed citations; self-hostable, model-agnostic, SOC 2 Type II
why_tier: Cross-SaaS search + graph + cited answers + agents = Astell's core mechanics; 'own your infra' open-source angle is the wedge against Glean/Astell
notes: Apache-2.0 OSS with paid enterprise tier; funding/stage/founding unconfirmed (stage=bootstrapped is a guess)

name: Jedify
what: 'Autonomous context graph' / Semantic Fusion — an AI-ready contextual data layer fusing business logic over warehouses, BI tools, databases and CRMs so agents reason with full company context
why_tier: Tanderrum-class: Astell's context-graph promise in warehouse/BI vocabulary; one connector-set or positioning shift away from the work-tool lane
notes: $24M Series A (Norwest lead, Snowflake Ventures strategic; ~$33M total); HQ inferred from Israeli press (Calcalist/Ctech), founding unconfirmed

name: Doti
what: DIY agentic enterprise-search platform that searches a team's tools and builds workflow agents; acquired by Salesforce (announced Nov 13 2025, closed Q4 FY2026) to power agentic search in Slack/Agentforce
why_tier: Direct-lane enterprise search; acquisition = 4th consolidation of the category into a suite (after Dashworks→HubSpot, Unleash→Zendesk, Akooda→Tulip)
notes: Customers Orca Security, Similarweb, Bringg; HQ inferred from Israeli customer base; founding/funding undisclosed
